poem:
The soft bedding 
The dying glow of the evening 
The slight hint of rain in the skies
The thunders and the clouds that collide
A cold gentle breeze..
That make the curtains dance in rhythms and rhymes..
This little place is my piece of us
Creating a Peace that's US

The cold nights are warm because 
I feel your body on mine..
Tangled limbs and teasing smiles..
Soft hums and whispered lullabies..
Oh my heavens..
This is the sweetest sweetest lieeee...

Your soft hair ..
My  hand running through them curls..
The breaths creating a divine symphony
Soft snores ....and meaningless whines
Oh my heavens...
This is all just so divine..

I smile a lot these days 
Tend to notice and acknowledge 
The rustling of leaves 
And the dew-bathed ways
Was it always this beautiful?
Or was I just being ignorant ?
ofcourse I  silently cherished the skies
The colors and the thunders
The lightning and the blunders 
But nowadays ,
I talk a little more 
Maybe way too much 
Abt this and that 
Well, It's nothing special as such 
The stories i carve 
Tangled threads need not to be solved 
As chaotic as the weather 
Sometimes a little sombre 
And a dangerous jubilation all together 
I like how it is 
Maybe its just you 
That makes me go all through 
Honestly,  I don't mind 
Just stay 
Okay darling?

The gyrations of the bodies around me
Blend into a purplish hallow
Kinda similar to the color of the dress
Molding all around you 
It's my definition of perfection 

The way you tip your head back 
Body vibrating with the force of your 
Sparkingly blinding laughs..
Ohh i just wanna sit and master you...
Take you in and blend it within..
Memorise you , Cherish it from far away..

Don't need the shots tonight babe
I am already all hazy
The waves you paint all around
With those lush hips and crafted legs..
I am so convinced...
Heaven fucking exists...

Red , blue , white and yellow..
I feel all of them deep in my bones
The explosion of colors on a black canvas..
The ultimate masterpiece 
The Supreme piece of art..
You are the personification of the starry nights
Scientists crave and lovers cry for..
The band of spectrum 
Which beholds it all..

Is it too much? 
Have been thinkìng about it 
Everytime this ends with
 Admiring you 
For every petty thing that you do 
Cursing you 
Trillion thoughts fighting for hope
Maybe its already a lost battle
Maybe you are already mine 
Maybe its just a matter of time
Then it will be just you me and the sunshines..

Is it too much?? 
The agony you make me go through..
I am filled with violets and whites
And suddenly its the darkest of blues ..
What if 
I want all of them equally intertwined  
Imperfect and submerged 
And leave it to the world  
To find the difference...

Is it too much??
You making me dream 
The prettiest truth 
U and me and the evening lights 
Walks alongside the rivers 
These starry nights 
Its too good to be perfect 
You pinch my check 
Forcing me into the ugliest lie 
Where you are so close 
Yet miles away 
Where i just keep falling for you 
And you remain 
Aloof of this scene 
Leaving me in utter 
Dismay..
But oh darling 
What can i say?
I am shamelessly hopeful ;
In the darkest night 
You are my only moon

I tumble everytime I glance in your
direction
Wild , wild thoughts provoking bad decisions
It's like the infamous story of moth and the flames
You are everything I shouldn't want 
But m still too tempted to play risky games
You are the combination of hot red on pitch black
Too beautiful to resist yet dangerous to have 

People often define me as placid
Mistakingly so
But everytime you give me those eyes
I am wrapped around a red hallow
You are the addiction; 
I never ever want to be free off
You are the chaos to my calm
Just like the silence before a storm
I should refrain from indulging in such thoughts
But the instance your name is mentioned,
It explodes like fireworks ; My resolve

You are the fire and i want to mold with the flames
Painting us in the shades of angry red and hot orange
I will have it sooner or later
Nothing to worry darling ..
It's what you have secretly catered

I promise to keep you in the confines of my soul
Break my resolve and bend to have you close
It's almost ridiculous if you ask me
But you are mine for keeps.

Withering leaves
Snowy streets 
Blinking lights 
And misty nights
The reddish purple glow 
Of your cheeks 
Highlighting your little nose 
Adding a glaze to your already sparkling eyes ...
Soft pink lips giving way to pearly white teeth
U look like a dream 
Showing your dimples off
I feel myself melting
Like the snow near the furnace 
Torturous and slow ..
Winters resemble you in every way 
So beautiful in yourself..
The cold seems familiar 
Has me craving for your warmth against my body
Holding me so close 
I can feel your heart beat 
Under the thick sheets ...
I am like this snow and you are the coniferous ..
Making me look beautiful 
Making me feel loved ...
And I am obsessed 
More than i can admit 
I am never letting you go 
As the coniferous does for the snow ..

The pattern of words that you use 
Frequently to converse 
I notice them
I remember them 
The lazy way you walk 
Has me craving to be your company 
Beacuse for you I am willing to be the epitome of being lazy 
The thoughts crowd and rustle in 
Everytime forcing their way in my mind 
Its unhealthy, really 
The amount you occupy of my head space 
Its growing on me 
The infatuation of you 
Intoxicates my mind 
Takes over making everything else sublime 
I shamelessly fabricate 
Every intricate detail of the possibility 
Of us being real 
The consequences this situation will rear 
You gotta trust me on this
I have no ounce of fear 
All I know is you consume me 
You are my brand new hobby 
Fuck the slow burnings
I am craving for hot and fast happenings 
But this "LIKE -LOVE "thing 
It's so cruel and selfish 
But oh , 
Don't  I just love 
Every bit of it!!!

Just yet another day 
Of you doing just normal things 
And my very faithful heart 
Goes betraying me yet again 
Just fluttering at ease 
At the smile on your face 
At that little crease in your cheek 

Just yet another month 
Passes in a dreamy haze 
With me glowing every time 
You walk by 
Unacknowledged 
Of me and other little things 

Just like that 
Here comes the end of the year 
I still live a beautiful dream 
Of you being mine 
The urge takes over me 
To pour all of this out 
But 
Nevermind ..
And you walk towards me 
Looking like a starry night 
And just then 
I know m far gone 
Far gone to look behind

What would you say? 
If i asked you to stay?
Alongside the sunsets 
And the tides..
What would you think ?
If i say , you are my forever and everafter?
Just like the dews and the sweet evenings..
What would you do?
If i pulled you little closer?
If we forget everything that keeps us 
Apart??
Go crazy , just like the frantic beats 
Of our hearts ..
Feel the burn and the ice all at once..
What would you say?
If we get tangled and twisted 
Along the way?
No matter if we walk , run or crawl 
We would do it together 
Always , forever and ever after...
Let me tell you 
You are the universe i dwell in 
Without you 
I am close to nothing .

Nestled
beneath the calm of your hold

Morning’s light
Folding around us

The scent of you
Carried in the stillness

You look at me
Eyes soft, gazing

My skin remembers
where your hands have been

Silent vows
pressed
Onto my body

Colours,
Staining skin

Blues, purples and yellows

It feels so good
Everything does
It’s almost frightening

The warmth lingers
But time does not hesitate

Hold me a moment more

Let my eyes speak
The adoration
My heart keeps for you

The day pulls me away
I let it,
Unwilling

is even more fun than going to San Sebastian, Irún, Hendaye, Biarritz, Bayonne
or being sick to my stomach on the Travesera de Gracia in Barcelona
partly because in your orange shirt you look like a better happier St. Sebastian
partly because of my love for you, partly because of your love for yoghurt
partly because of the fluorescent orange tulips around the birches
partly because of the secrecy our smiles take on before people and statuary
it is hard to believe when I’m with you that there can be anything as still
as solemn as unpleasantly definitive as statuary when right in front of it
in the warm New York 4 o’clock light we are drifting back and forth
between each other like a tree breathing through its spectacles

and the portrait show seems to have no faces in it at all, just paint
you suddenly wonder why in the world anyone ever did them

I look
at you and I would rather look at you than all the portraits in the world
except possibly for the Polish Rider occasionally and anyway it’s in the Frick
which thank heavens you haven’t gone to yet so we can go together the first time
and the fact that you move so beautifully more or less takes care of Futurism
just as at home I never think of the Nude Descending a Staircase or
at a rehearsal a single drawing of Leonardo or Michelangelo that used to wow me
and what good does all the research of the Impressionists do them
when they never got the right person to stand near the tree when the sun sank
or for that matter Marino Marini when he didn’t pick the rider as carefully
as the horse

it seems they were all cheated of some marvelous experience
which is not going to go wasted on me which is why I am telling you about it

How can I keep my soul in me, so that
it doesn't touch your soul? How can I raise
it high enough, past you, to other things?
I would like to shelter it, among remote
lost objects, in some dark and silent place
that doesn't resonate when your depths resound.
Yet everything that touches us, me and you,
takes us together like a violin's bow,
which draws one voice out of two separate strings.
Upon what instrument are we two spanned?
And what musician holds us in his hand?
Oh sweetest song.

More than a catbird hates a cat,
Or a criminal hates a clue,
Or the Axis hates the United States,
That's how much I love you.

I love you more than a duck can swim,
And more than a grapefruit squirts,
I love you more than gin rummy is a bore,
And more than a toothache hurts.

As a shipwrecked sailor hates the sea,
Or a juggler hates a shove,
As a hostess detests unexpected guests,
That's how much you I love.

I love you more than a wasp can sting,
And more than the subway jerks,
I love you as much as a beggar needs a crutch,
And more than a hangnail irks.

I swear to you by the stars above,
And below, if such there be,
As the High Court loathes perjurious oaths,
That's how you're loved by me.

This was the first time I knelt  
and with my lips, frightened, kissed  
the lit inwardly pink petaled lips.

It was like touching a bird's exposed heart
with your tongue. 

Summer dawn flowing into the room parting the
curtains—the lamps dimming—breeze
rendered visible. Lightning,
                             and then soft applause
from the leaves . . .

Almost children, we lay asleep in love listening to the 
rain.

We didn't ask to be born.

So, we'll go no more a roving

So late into the night,

Though the heart be still as loving,

And the moon be still as bright.

 

For the sword outwears its sheath,

And the soul wears out the breast,

And the heart must pause to breathe,

And love itself have rest.

 

Though the night was made for loving,

And the day returns too soon,

Yet we'll go no more a roving

By the light of the moon.

Let us tunnel
Through the rubble,

Through the thrum.
Let us rut through the sum

Of who we were,
Or are,

Or will be in the years to come:
A couple

Of someones
Who used to be in love.

Used to be in love.
Ho. Hum.

These days: Seem to be in hate.
Gypsum, marble, pyrite, slate.

See here. A pit of snakes.
Look there. The rock of your rages.

And I’m in a cable-cage, slinking down your shaft.
You fondle that hefty What if. . . ? as if

To hurl it. All the other holes
Are blatant hells.

A dragline scrapes our fossicked floor.
I am the ether. You are the ore.

This is the war that nobody won.
Like afterdamp collapsing a lung.

You take to swinging a pickaxe.
I take back my vamping kinks

And the pavement beneath us sinks.
This stinks. Think: In-situ leaching

But with leeches, louses,
Lampreys. Oh Spouse,

Your hard hat leaks a surfeit
Of lamp rays that’s wasted sub-surface.

A night so pitch it’s perfectly black.
A sapphire scarred by a scratch.

Sickness, health, abundance, lack.
The salt in my wound. The shirt off your back.

So our bloodcup runs empty of urge.
The metallurgy

We’re made of demands its dirge.
Our burrows diverge.

Our passages split.
Copper, silver, gravel, grit.

Am I—perhaps—alluvial?
Un-live-with-able?

A bit too simple or silty?
Only gold really ought be gilty.

And you are as cold as coal.
I am your dole, your lode,

Your carbon-flawed diamond.
All told: We drilled and hit demons.

Granite, though, is good for graves.
Granted, a mine isn’t quite a cave.

What isn’t mine, I cannot give.

My own dear love, he is strong and bold
      And he cares not what comes after.
His words ring sweet as a chime of gold,
      And his eyes are lit with laughter.
He is jubilant as a flag unfurled—
      Oh, a girl, she’d not forget him.
My own dear love, he is all my world,—
      And I wish I’d never met him.

My love, he’s mad, and my love, he’s fleet,
      And a wild young wood-thing bore him!
The ways are fair to his roaming feet,
      And the skies are sunlit for him.
As sharply sweet to my heart he seems
      As the fragrance of acacia.
My own dear love, he is all my dreams,—
      And I wish he were in Asia.

My love runs by like a day in June,
      And he makes no friends of sorrows.
He’ll tread his galloping rigadoon
      In the pathway of the morrows.
He’ll live his days where the sunbeams start,
      Nor could storm or wind uproot him.
My own dear love, he is all my heart,—
      And I wish somebody’d shoot him.

She walks in beauty, like the night
Of cloudless climes and starry skies;
And all that’s best of dark and bright
Meet in her aspect and her eyes;
Thus mellowed to that tender light
Which heaven to gaudy day denies.

One shade the more, one ray the less,
Had half impaired the nameless grace
Which waves in every raven tress,
Or softly lightens o’er her face;
Where thoughts serenely sweet express,
How pure, how dear their dwelling-place.

And on that cheek, and o’er that brow,
So soft, so calm, yet eloquent,
The smiles that win, the tints that glow,
But tell of days in goodness spent,
A mind at peace with all below,
A heart whose love is innocent!

love is more thicker than forget
more thinner than recall
more seldom than a wave is wet
more frequent than to fail

it is most mad and moonly
and less it shall unbe
than all the sea which only
is deeper than the sea

love is less always than to win
less never than alive
less bigger than the least begin
less littler than forgive

it is most sane and sunly
and more it cannot die
than all the sky which only
is higher than the sky

O my Luve is like a red, red rose
   That’s newly sprung in June;
O my Luve is like the melody
   That’s sweetly played in tune.

So fair art thou, my bonnie lass,
   So deep in luve am I;
And I will luve thee still, my dear,
   Till a’ the seas gang dry.

Till a’ the seas gang dry, my dear,
   And the rocks melt wi’ the sun;
I will love thee still, my dear,
   While the sands o’ life shall run.

And fare thee weel, my only luve!
   And fare thee weel awhile!
And I will come again, my luve,
   Though it were ten thousand mile.

Lipstick is one of the very finest elixirs
It has a soft consistency
And can write on glass
Red rum is sweet
And candied
With cherries from the
Bottom of the bottle
We sip it so gracefully
I am in love again
His name is something
But who can remember
Memory is a lake
And all of that
I can’t remember
Where I left my last exercise
I was only ever sitting here
Watching the neon burn out
I have taken my red lipstick
And written Help across my chest
He looks at it so kindly
I know I am real again
I can feel the sweet bitters
Of the drink make me into a woman
I am not crying
I am writing sonnets in my mind
About all of the vacations
We will take after this one
I throw the glass against the wall
The only thing that matters in love is passion

A glimpse through an interstice caught,
Of a crowd of workmen and drivers in a bar-room around the stove late of a winter night, and I unremark’d seated in a corner,
Of a youth who loves me and whom I love, silently approaching and seating himself near, that he may hold me by the hand,
A long while amid the noises of coming and going, of drinking and oath and smutty jest,
There we two, content, happy in being together, speaking little, perhaps not a word.

I love your lips when they’re wet with wine
And red with a wild desire;
I love your eyes when the lovelight lies
Lit with a passionate fire.
I love your arms when the warm white flesh
Touches mine in a fond embrace;
I love your hair when the strands enmesh
Your kisses against my face.

Not for me the cold, calm kiss
Of a virgin’s bloodless love;
Not for me the saint’s white bliss,
Nor the heart of a spotless dove.
But give me the love that so freely gives
And laughs at the whole world’s blame,
With your body so young and warm in my arms,
It sets my poor heart aflame.

So kiss me sweet with your warm wet mouth,
Still fragrant with ruby wine,
And say with a fervor born of the South
That your body and soul are mine.
Clasp me close in your warm young arms,
While the pale stars shine above,
And we’ll live our whole young lives away
In the joys of a living love.

round
my neck
an amu-
let
Be-
tween
my eyes
a star
A
ring
in my
nose
and a
gold
chain
to
Keep me
where
You
are

How Love came in, I do not know,
Whether by th’ eye, or eare, or no:
Or whether with the soule it came
(At first) infused with the same:
Whether in part ‘tis here or there,
Or, like the soule, whole every where:
This troubles me: but as I well
As any other, this can tell;
That when from hence she does depart,
The out-let then is from the heart.

The thin cool fingers of the wind
Caress your tall loveliness;
The wind kisses
Each shining strand of spun brightness
About your head
Then sends a shimmering waterfall
About the face of you;
I think the summer sun
Would be jealous of your hair,
O Golden Goddess,
Did he not know you.

As for me
I have known you through long yearning years;
Ages ago I built a home for you
Within my mind
A home where I have lived with you
So that when you came down to me
Tired of Olympus,
O Golden Goddess,
I already knew how you would be.

And yet I did not know—
For not even the clearest dream
Can equal the dazzling reality of you.
There is no way to think
The wedding of your lips with mine;
Imagination makes no magic
To match the roaring wonder
Of you close to me;
And now that you have come
My dream caught and clothed in flesh
I shall not let you go.

I shall make you part of me,
My darling,
Fundamental as heart
Primary as mind
And to you I shall become
As the blood in your veins
So that neither you nor I
Could survive
The mutilation of leaving.

Could we today
See the first atom created
Looking on as it grew into a universe
Suns burn and blossom, hurling off whole worlds
Spinning stars and planets grow tired, cold and die
Matter disintegrates
All that is become again one hapless atom
Then vanish
There still would not be time enough
To satisfy my love for you …

When you are old and grey and full of sleep,
And nodding by the fire, take down this book,
And slowly read, and dream of the soft look
Your eyes had once, and of their shadows deep;

How many loved your moments of glad grace,
And loved your beauty with love false or true,
But one man loved the pilgrim soul in you,
And loved the sorrows of your changing face;

And bending down beside the glowing bars,
Murmur, a little sadly, how Love fled
And paced upon the mountains overhead
And hid his face amid a crowd of stars.

when i think of us i think of the lakewater
near longtown, what might not technically
constitute a lake but i prefer that word for
the open mouth of its vowel, how it called
us to its throat & held us there, in the sun,
the high points of our faces slick with light
& its arc around our shoulders, the soft
gathering of flesh around our knees,
the lone chair we found near the shore
where we took turns posing, jutting out
an eloquent hip, cackling in the bright language
of flowers for whom i downloaded an app
& learned their names: beautyberry, yarrow,
cornus florida, black-eyed susan, & you,
& you, my bright hibiscus, my every color

I really need YOU....

I want to memorize the precise shade
Of brown that your eyes turn in evening's fade.
I want to learn the cadence of your speech,
Keep every little lesson you can teach.
My life's a study, and the subject's you,
In everything you are, and all you do.

Your love is not a harbor, safe and sound,
But a wild ocean where I gladly drowned.
It's not a shelter from the wind and rain,
But the thrilling, sweet, and terrifying hurricane.
I do not come to you to be at peace,
But for the beautiful, chaotic release.

Sometimes I watch you when you do not see,
Just going on about your day, carefree.
A simple gesture, how you sip your tea,
And a wave of adoration washes over me.
How did I live before this perfect view?
How did I ever breathe, not loving you?

My mind’s a frantic, busy city street,
With worried thoughts and hurried, anxious feet.
But when you hold me, everything grows still,
Upon a silent, solitary hill.
You are the quiet I have longed to find,
The peaceful center of my restless mind.

They speak of passion as a burning fire,
A wild and all-consuming, mad desire.
But what we have is like the sun's warm light,
Consistent, steady, gentle, and so bright.
It doesn't burn, it doesn't rage or fade,
It simply is, the promise we have made.

It’s not the lightning, not the crashing storm,
But just the simple feeling of your form.
The steady rhythm of your sleeping breath,
A quiet shield against all thoughts of death.
You are the anchor in my drifting sea,
The calm, the peace, the certainty.

I read my favorite lines from my old books,
And find your meaning in the author's looks.
I hear a melody upon the air,
And trace the notes back to the way you care.
It seems that everything I love to do,
Has somehow found its way to lead to you.

Your laughter is a sudden, summer shower,
That breaks the heat within a single hour.
It washes over all my doubts and fears,
The sweetest music I have heard in years.
So laugh again, and let the sound cascade,
For in that joy, my own happiness is made.

I used to build my walls up, stone by stone,
A fortress where I chose to be alone.
But with a single smile, you found the key,
And walked right in, dismantling all of me.
Now in the ruins, something starts to grow,
A home for two, a place I want to know.

My skin remembers where your fingers traced
The constellations on my sleeping waist.
It holds the memory, a silent art,
A map of you imprinted on my heart.
A touch so fleeting, yet it leaves a sign,
A silent vow that tells me you are mine.

Morning coffee, a familiar haze,
The quiet comfort of our shared gaze.
No grand pronouncements, no dramatic plea,
Just your hand resting gently on my knee.
In this small silence, everything is clear,
My favorite part of day, with you right here.

The world was painted in monochrome grey,
A silent film of passing, hurried days.
Then you arrived, a brushstroke, bold and true,
And suddenly my world was drenched in hue.
You are the color my heart learned to see,
The vibrant, living thing inside of me.
